title: salix × sandomiriensis
intro: Salix × sandomiriensis, also known as the Sandomir willow, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Salix genus, derived from the crossing of Salix alba and Salix fragilis. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in salicylic acid, a compound that has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The salicylic acid in Salix × sandomiriensis has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. Salix × sandomiriensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health issues.
